items.title: Lead Product Management
items.description: ResponsibilitiesAssess the Market Demand for Each Product Variant.Benchmarking of Competitors – Cells & Modules.Technology Roadmap for Highest Efficiency.Technical Pre Sales & Post Sales - Ensure Customer requirements are met through Product offerings and liaise with the plant.Best Cost Vs Quality Matrix – Materials, Process, Generation, Reliability, TTFProduct Certification of PV Modules – All Markets.SPOC for Generation Assessment of Various Modules at different customer location –Domestic & International Customers.SPOC for receiving customer complaints & Assessment – Ensure Complaint Closure within time with RCA & Replacements.SPOC for bankability, Lab, customer, ALMM, traceability audits etc.Coordination with manufacturing team for sample preparation & submission to labLCOE comparison for different cell & module technologiesTechnical Awareness session for sales & channel partnersProduct Management MISQualificationsB.E. / B.Tech with 8 to 12 years of relevant work experience
items.descriptionHtml:
Responsibilities

  • Assess the Market Demand for Each Product Variant.
  • Benchmarking of Competitors – Cells & Modules.
  • Technology Roadmap for Highest Efficiency.
  • Technical Pre Sales & Post Sales - Ensure Customer requirements are met through Product offerings and liaise with the plant.
  • Best Cost Vs Quality Matrix – Materials, Process, Generation, Reliability, TTF
  • Product Certification of PV Modules – All Markets.
  • SPOC for Generation Assessment of Various Modules at different customer location –Domestic & International Customers.
  • SPOC for receiving customer complaints & Assessment – Ensure Complaint Closure within time with RCA & Replacements.
  • SPOC for bankability, Lab, customer, ALMM, traceability audits etc.
  • Coordination with manufacturing team for sample preparation & submission to lab
  • LCOE comparison for different cell & module technologies
  • Technical Awareness session for sales & channel partners
  • Product Management MIS

Qualifications

B.E. / B.Tech with 8 to 12 years of relevant work experience

Decide on product enhancements or discontinuation when necessary.Customer Feedback And Product ImprovementCollect and prioritize customer feedback to enhance product features. Continuously refine products to improve customer satisfaction and market fit.Product Performance TrackingTrack product performance using KPIs, sales data, and feedback. Optimize product performance to increase market share and customer satisfaction.Team Leadership And DevelopmentLead and mentor the product management team, fostering collaboration, innovation, and accountability. Support team growth to achieve organizational and personal goals.Product Marketing CollaborationCollaborate with marketing teams on product launch strategies, promotional campaigns, and customer education to drive adoption and market penetration.Key Stakeholders - InternalHead - Product ManagementSales TeamMarketing TeamSupply Chain/Logistics TeamR&D/Innovation TeamKey Stakeholders - ExternalVendors/SuppliersCustomers (for product feedback)External Consultants/Industry ExpertsRegulatory BodiesQualificationsEducational Qualification:Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Business Administration, or a related field. Demonstrated success in product development, positioning, and strategy, especially within the solar or energy sector.
items.descriptionHtml:
About Business

JOB DESCRIPTION

Adani Group: Adani Group is a diversified organisation in India comprising 10 publicly traded companies. It has created a world class logistics and utility infrastructure portfolio that has a pan-India presence. Adani Group is headquartered in Ahmedabad, in the state of Gujarat, India. Over the years, Adani Group has positioned itself to be the market leader in its logistics and energy businesses focusing on large scale infrastructure development in India with O & M practices benchmarked to global standards. With four IG rated businesses, it is the only Infrastructure Investment Grade issuer in India.

Adani Solar:Adani Solar is India's 1st and largest vertically integrated solar PV manufacturer with 4 GW cells & modules and 2 GW of ingots & wafers manufacturing capacities

Job Purpose:The Lead - Product Management is responsible for overseeing the entire product lifecycle, ensuring that products meet customer needs and market demands. This role involves developing product strategies, collaborating with internal teams, and managing product performance. The Lead - Product Management is also accountable for ensuring product roadmaps are aligned with business objectives, while driving innovation and efficiency within the product development process.

Responsibilities

Product Strategy and Vision:

  • Develop and execute the product strategy, aligning with company goals. Define product vision, objectives, and KPIs to drive growth and innovation.

Market Research And Analysis

  • Conduct market research to understand customer needs, market trends, and competitive landscape. Use insights to guide product decisions and identify opportunities.

Product Development Lifecycle

  • Manage the full product lifecycle, from ideation to launch. Ensure timely product releases and collaborate with cross-functional teams for successful execution.

Product Positioning And Pricing

  • Define product positioning, messaging, and pricing strategies in collaboration with sales and marketing teams to meet customer needs and stay competitive.

Cross-functional Collaboration

  • Work closely with sales, marketing, engineering, and customer support teams to ensure alignment on product development and customer experience.

Product Lifecycle Management

  • Oversee product improvements and manage updates based on customer feedback and market trends. Decide on product enhancements or discontinuation when necessary.

Customer Feedback And Product Improvement

  • Collect and prioritize customer feedback to enhance product features. Continuously refine products to improve customer satisfaction and market fit.

Product Performance Tracking

  • Track product performance using KPIs, sales data, and feedback. Optimize product performance to increase market share and customer satisfaction.

Team Leadership And Development

  • Lead and mentor the product management team, fostering collaboration, innovation, and accountability. Support team growth to achieve organizational and personal goals.

Product Marketing Collaboration

  • Collaborate with marketing teams on product launch strategies, promotional campaigns, and customer education to drive adoption and market penetration.

Key Stakeholders - Internal

  • Head - Product Management
  • Sales Team
  • Marketing Team
  • Supply Chain/Logistics Team
  • R&D/Innovation Team

Key Stakeholders - External

  • Vendors/Suppliers
  • Customers (for product feedback)
  • External Consultants/Industry Experts
  • Regulatory Bodies

Qualifications

Educational Qualification:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Business Administration, or a related field. MBA or equivalent product management certification is a plus.

Work Experience (Range Of Years)

  • Min 8-12 years of experience in product management, with at least 3-5 years in a leadership or managerial role. Demonstrated success in product development, positioning, and strategy, especially within the solar or energy sector.

items.title: Director of Product Management

We empower our customers with lights-out automation for their mission-critical business processes.ABOUT US Redwood Software is the leading orchestration platform for the autonomous enterprise, driving business transformation at the lowest total cost of ownership. Redwood empowers organizations to intelligently automate and orchestrate mission-critical business and IT processes across complex ERP, hybrid cloud, data and emerging agentic AI systems. Through its SaaS-first automation fabric—with AI embedded across the automation lifecycle—Redwood accelerates the path to autonomous operations. Backed by 30 years of experience and trusted by more than 50% of the Fortune 50, Redwood helps organizations unlock human potential to focus on innovation, growth and what’s next.CORE VALUESOne Team. One Redwood.Make Your Own WeatherObsess over Customer SuccessWork the ProblemBe CuriousOwn the OutcomeRespect Each OtherYOUR IMPACTAs the Product Leader you will own the product strategy, customer retention, and migration roadmap for Redwood's legacy workload automation portfolio. You will maximize customer lifetime value, and position migrations as expansion opportunities—not churn events.Cross-Functional LeadershipPartner with Product team to ensure migration blockers are items.descriptionHtml:
OUR MISSION

At Redwood Software we unleash human potential. We empower our customers with lights-out automation for their mission-critical business processes.


ABOUT US

Redwood Software is the leading orchestration platform for the autonomous enterprise, driving business transformation at the lowest total cost of ownership. Redwood empowers organizations to intelligently automate and orchestrate mission-critical business and IT processes across complex ERP, hybrid cloud, data and emerging agentic AI systems. Through its SaaS-first automation fabric—with AI embedded across the automation lifecycle—Redwood accelerates the path to autonomous operations. Backed by 30 years of experience and trusted by more than 50% of the Fortune 50, Redwood helps organizations unlock human potential to focus on innovation, growth and what’s next.


CORE VALUES

One Team. One Redwood.

Make Your Own Weather

Obsess over Customer Success

Work the Problem

Be Curious

Own the Outcome

Respect Each Other


YOUR IMPACT

As the Product Leader you will own the product strategy, customer retention, and migration roadmap for Redwood's legacy workload automation portfolio. You will maximize customer lifetime value, and position migrations as expansion opportunities—not churn events.

Cross-Functional Leadership

  • Partner with Product team to ensure migration blockers are addressed in the roadmap (feature parity, migration utilities, customer-specific integrations)
  • Collaborate with Engineering to prioritize defect fixes, security patches, and compliance requirements while minimizing investment in legacy platforms
  • Work with Customer Success to design migration engagement models: discovery workshops, migration planning, technical validation, go-live support
  • Support Sales with migration business cases, competitive positioning

Team Leadership & Mentorship

  • Develop Product Owners responsible for backlog management, sprint ceremonies, and delivery execution
  • Model product management best practices customer-centric discovery, data-driven prioritization, outcome-based roadmapping, transparent communication
  • Build PO capability in migration management customer segmentation, churn risk assessment, migration planning, technical enablement


YOUR EXPERIENCE

Required Experience:

  • 5–7 years in product management with demonstrated track record of shipping complex B2B enterprise software
  • Legacy product management experience, Sustained platforms while building migration paths to modern architectures
  • Customer retention expertise: Prevented churn during platform transitions, positioned migrations as value-add vs. disruption
  • Enterprise software domain knowledge: Workload automation, ERP integration, cloud infrastructure, or mission-critical operations software preferred
  • Cross-functional leadership: Partnered with Engineering, Sales, CSM, and Support to balance competing priorities and deliver customer outcomes
  • Analytical rigor: Used data (renewal rates, support ticket trends, usage telemetry) to inform prioritization and predict churn risk

Preferred Experience:

  • 8+ years in product management with portfolio-level accountability for multiple products or customer segments
  • Migration strategy execution: Led customer migrations at scale (100+ accounts), developed segmentation models, built migration tooling/playbooks
  • Workload automation or IT operations domain. Deep understanding of job scheduling, batch processing, ERP integration, SAP workflows, or cloud orchestration
  • Team leadership Hired, developed, and retained Product Owners or junior PMs in distributed/offshore models

items.descriptionHtml:
About ADA

ADA is the Data and AI Experience Company. Present in 14 markets globally, we build intelligent experiences that power enterprise growth, spanning identity and authentication, personalization, commerce, and data & AI foundations; where every touchpoint earns trust, every interaction creates value, and every decision runs on real-time intelligence, so enterprises can stay ahead and deliver measurable outcomes. Our mission is to build the world's most intelligent growth platform for every client we serve. We work with over 1,500 global brands across CPG, Retail, Telco, BFSI, and Healthcare.

Job Description: Head of AI

Role Overview

We are seeking a Head of AI to lead the design, development, and deployment of AI/ML capabilities across our core products. This role sits at the intersection of product innovation, engineering execution, and applied AI strategy, reporting directly to the CTO.

In addition to driving AI excellence within products, this role will contribute (~10% bandwidth) to the ADA’s AI strategy, helping shape high-impact AI integrations

AI Strategy & Leadership

Define and own the AI/ML vision and roadmap aligned with business and product strategy

Identify high-leverage opportunities to embed AI-driven capabilities across products

Drive adoption of modern AI paradigms (LLMs, agentic workflows, RAG, etc.)

Establish best practices for Responsible AI, evaluation, and governance

Product AI Development

Lead end-to-end development of AI/ML features across multiple products

Translate business problems into scalable AI solutions

Ensure production-grade scalability and reliability

Drive AI-first product thinking across teams

Architecture & Technical Excellence

Define scalable architecture for ML pipelines and LLM orchestration

Establish standards for experimentation, monitoring, and data pipelines

Guide build vs buy decisions across AI stack

Implement robust evaluation and observability frameworks

Team Building & Leadership

Build and lead a high-performing team of ML Engineers, Applied Scientists, and AI Engineers

Mentor teams on advanced AI techniques and system design

Foster a culture of innovation, ownership, and experimentation

Consulting / Solutions Contribution (~10%)

Support consulting engagements with AI expertise

Review solution architectures and proposals

Participate in key client discussions as an AI SME

Translate internal capabilities into external AI solutions

Cross-Functional Collaboration

Work closely with CTO, Product, Engineering, and Business stakeholders

Act as the AI thought leader across the organization

Required Qualifications

10–15+ years of experience in AI/ML or Applied AI Engineering

Experience building and deploying AI/ML systems in production

Strong expertise in ML, LLMs, or agentic systems

Proven leadership in cross-functional teams

Strong system design and architectural skills

Preferred Qualifications

Experience building AI-powered products

Familiarity with agentic frameworks and LLM evaluation systems

Exposure to domains like e-commerce, operations, or conversational AI

Key Traits

Product-first mindset

Strong ownership and execution bias

Ability to operate across strategy and execution

Clear communication skills

Pragmatic decision-making

Success Metrics

Impact of AI features on product KPIs

Speed and quality of AI deployments

Scalability and robustness of AI systems

Consulting impact and client satisfaction

Growth and strength of AI team

Reporting Structure

Reports to: Chief Technology Officer (CTO)

Collaborates with Product, Engineering, and Consulting teams

By submitting this, you agree to this Privacy Notice and you will be deemed to have consented to the collection, use, and disclosure of your Personal Information in accordance with this Privacy Notice: https://adaglobal.com/privacy-policy/

Unfortunately, we are only able to contact shortlisted applicants. We encourage you to continuously visit our website www.adaglobal.com for regular updates on available roles

We transform businesses using data, AI and tech | ADA

Pioneers in data and analytics, we are powering global marketing and commerce digital transformation with data and AI-led impact. Learn more here!

items.title: Senior Manager - Security
items.description: Responsibilities2) Guard Force & Vendor Management3) Incident Response, Investigations & Loss Prevention4) Compliance, Audits & Regulatory Liaison5) Crisis Management & Emergency Preparedness6) People, Culture & Awareness7) Digital & Data-Driven SecuritySite Security Operations & GovernanceOwn the site security risk assessment; implement layered controls for perimeter, gates, yards, warehouses, high-value rooms, control rooms, and substations.Run daily security operations (24x7)—muster/briefing, deployment, patrolling, post orders, incident logs, daily reports.Ensure effective access control (employees, contractors, visitors) and material movement control (inward/outward, gate-pass, returnable/non-returnable).Oversee electronic security systems: CCTV, VMS, ACS, boom barriers, turnstiles, intrusion alarms, ANPR, command center—uptime, health checks, audits, and analytics review.Implement key management, badge management, and segregation-of-duties for critical zones.Establish and enforce Security SOPs, Post Orders, Emergency Response Plans, and Business Continuity/Disaster Recovery linkages with plant operations and EHS.Lead and optimize a blended model of in-house & outsourced guard force (supervisors, guards, QRT, dog squad as applicable).Define SLAs/ KPIs, conduct monthly vendor reviews, manage rosters, overtime, training, and discipline.Drive cost optimization without compromising risk posture; support RFPs, commercial evaluations, and AMC renewals for security systems.Lead incident response (theft, pilferage, sabotage, workplace violence, protest/flash-mob, utility disruptions, cyber-physical convergence issues).Conduct root-cause investigations with documented evidence handling; run corrective & preventive action (CAPA).Implement cargo/yard/warehouse security, seal integrity, weighbridge checks, and truck route monitoring to minimize shrinkage and fraud.Maintain case registers, dashboards, and management reporting.Ensure compliance with applicable industrial security, SEZ/port regulations, PASARA (for private security agencies), factory premises norms, and contractor compliance.Prepare for and lead internal/external audits; close findings on time.Liaise with local police, district administration, SEZ authorities, fire services, and disaster management agencies; maintain positive relationships.Maintain and test Emergency Response Plans for fire, flood/cyclone, chemical spill, power failure, social unrest, and medical emergencies; conduct mock drills with EHS.Run table-top exercises for leadership; ensure mass notification readiness and command center SOPs.Support executive protection and VIP movements on-site when required.Build a security-first culture through toolbox talks, induction, targeted awareness campaigns (tailgate security, badge discipline, photography restrictions, reporting suspicious activity).Coach the team on ethics, integrity, and respectful, lawful conduct with all stakeholders including contract labor.Leverage CCTV analytics, heatmaps, people/vehicle counting, and exception dashboards; track leading indicators.Partner with IT/OT/InfoSec for cyber-physical convergence: camera network segmentation, hardening of NVRs/servers, and access logs integrity.Maintain asset inventory and configuration baselines for security tech.QualificationsBachelor’s degree (Security Management, Criminology, Engineering, or related). Master’s preferred.12–18 years in security for large-scale manufacturing/industrial/port/SEZ environments; multi-plant campus experience preferred.
items.descriptionHtml:
Responsibilities2) Guard Force & Vendor Management3) Incident Response, Investigations & Loss Prevention4) Compliance, Audits & Regulatory Liaison5) Crisis Management & Emergency Preparedness6) People, Culture & Awareness7) Digital & Data-Driven Security

  • Site Security Operations & Governance
  • Own the site security risk assessment; implement layered controls for perimeter, gates, yards, warehouses, high-value rooms, control rooms, and substations.
  • Run daily security operations (24x7)—muster/briefing, deployment, patrolling, post orders, incident logs, daily reports.
  • Ensure effective access control (employees, contractors, visitors) and material movement control (inward/outward, gate-pass, returnable/non-returnable).
  • Oversee electronic security systems: CCTV, VMS, ACS, boom barriers, turnstiles, intrusion alarms, ANPR, command center—uptime, health checks, audits, and analytics review.
  • Implement key management, badge management, and segregation-of-duties for critical zones.
  • Establish and enforce Security SOPs, Post Orders, Emergency Response Plans, and Business Continuity/Disaster Recovery linkages with plant operations and EHS.
  • Lead and optimize a blended model of in-house & outsourced guard force (supervisors, guards, QRT, dog squad as applicable).
  • Define SLAs/ KPIs, conduct monthly vendor reviews, manage rosters, overtime, training, and discipline.
  • Drive cost optimization without compromising risk posture; support RFPs, commercial evaluations, and AMC renewals for security systems.
  • Lead incident response (theft, pilferage, sabotage, workplace violence, protest/flash-mob, utility disruptions, cyber-physical convergence issues).
  • Conduct root-cause investigations with documented evidence handling; run corrective & preventive action (CAPA).
  • Implement cargo/yard/warehouse security, seal integrity, weighbridge checks, and truck route monitoring to minimize shrinkage and fraud.
  • Maintain case registers, dashboards, and management reporting.
  • Ensure compliance with applicable industrial security, SEZ/port regulations, PASARA (for private security agencies), factory premises norms, and contractor compliance.
  • Prepare for and lead internal/external audits; close findings on time.
  • Liaise with local police, district administration, SEZ authorities, fire services, and disaster management agencies; maintain positive relationships.
  • Maintain and test Emergency Response Plans for fire, flood/cyclone, chemical spill, power failure, social unrest, and medical emergencies; conduct mock drills with EHS.
  • Run table-top exercises for leadership; ensure mass notification readiness and command center SOPs.
  • Support executive protection and VIP movements on-site when required.
  • Build a security-first culture through toolbox talks, induction, targeted awareness campaigns (tailgate security, badge discipline, photography restrictions, reporting suspicious activity).
  • Coach the team on ethics, integrity, and respectful, lawful conduct with all stakeholders including contract labor.
  • Leverage CCTV analytics, heatmaps, people/vehicle counting, and exception dashboards; track leading indicators.
  • Partner with IT/OT/InfoSec for cyber-physical convergence: camera network segmentation, hardening of NVRs/servers, and access logs integrity.
  • Maintain asset inventory and configuration baselines for security tech.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree (Security Management, Criminology, Engineering, or related). Master’s preferred.
  • 12–18 years in security for large-scale manufacturing/industrial/port/SEZ environments; multi-plant campus experience preferred.

items.title: Techno Commercial - System & IT
items.description: ResponsibilitiesManage all IT related procurement activities from HO and support TC team.Lead vendor negotiations to ensure cost-effective procurement and service agreements for IT systems and subscriptions.Manage, procure and renewal and optimize IT subscriptions, ensuring alignment with the business unit’s operational needs and strategic goals.Collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate IT solutions into ongoing infrastructure projects, including HAM model initiatives.Maintain up-to-date knowledge of emerging technologies and their applicability to infrastructure development.Support the TC Lead in achieving operational and strategic objectives through effective IT system management.QualificationsRequired Qualifications & ExperienceProven experience in vendor negotiation and IT subscription management, with a minimum of 2 years in a similar role.Demonstrated expertise in IT systems integration within infrastructure projects.Educational BackgroundBachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field.Preferred: Certifications in IT systems management or digital transformation.Years & Type Of ExperienceMinimum 2 years of experience in IT systems management and vendor management negotiations.Experience in infrastructure development projects is highly desirable.Domain ExpertiseIn-depth knowledge of IT systems and subscription management.Understanding of digital transformation strategies in large-scale operations in RMRW business.Digital & Operational ToolsProficiency in IT subscription management platforms.Experience with digital transformation tools and methodologies in TC department.Familiarity with project management software used in infrastructure development.
items.descriptionHtml:
Responsibilities

  • Manage all IT related procurement activities from HO and support TC team.
  • Lead vendor negotiations to ensure cost-effective procurement and service agreements for IT systems and subscriptions.
  • Manage, procure and renewal and optimize IT subscriptions, ensuring alignment with the business unit’s operational needs and strategic goals.
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate IT solutions into ongoing infrastructure projects, including HAM model initiatives.
  • Maintain up-to-date knowledge of emerging technologies and their applicability to infrastructure development.
  • Support the TC Lead in achieving operational and strategic objectives through effective IT system management.

Qualifications

Required Qualifications & Experience

  • Proven experience in vendor negotiation and IT subscription management, with a minimum of 2 years in a similar role.
  • Demonstrated expertise in IT systems integration within infrastructure projects.

Educational Background

  • Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field.
  • Preferred: Certifications in IT systems management or digital transformation.

Years & Type Of Experience

  • Minimum 2 years of experience in IT systems management and vendor management negotiations.
  • Experience in infrastructure development projects is highly desirable.

Domain Expertise

  • In-depth knowledge of IT systems and subscription management.
  • Understanding of digital transformation strategies in large-scale operations in RMRW business.

Digital & Operational Tools

  • Proficiency in IT subscription management platforms.
  • Experience with digital transformation tools and methodologies in TC department.
  • Familiarity with project management software used in infrastructure development.

items.title: Associate Officer - ILHBS
items.description: About Business JOB DESCRIPTION Adani Group: Adani Group is a diversified organisation in India comprising 10 publicly traded companies. It has created a world class logistics and utility infrastructure portfolio that has a pan-India presence. Adani Group is headquartered in Ahmedabad, in the state of Gujarat, India. Over the years, Adani Group has positioned itself to be the market leader in its logistics and energy businesses focusing on large scale infrastructure development in India with O & M practices benchmarked to global standards. With four IG rated businesses, it is the only Infrastructure Investment Grade issuer in India.Adani Airports Holding Limited: Adani Airports Holding Limited is reshaping the aviation landscape, transforming airports into dynamic hubs of connectivity, innovation, and service excellence. By prioritizing passenger experience, leveraging state-of-the-art technology, and enhancing operational efficiency, we are redefining airport infrastructure across India. Our vision is to create world-class airports that serve as gateways to regional development and global connectivity, positioning Adani Airports as a leader in the aviation sector with a focus on sustainable growth and community impact.Job Purpose: Screener is responsible to ensure the safe and efficient screening of hold baggage, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards and the detection of prohibited items or security threats. items.descriptionHtml:
About Business

 JOB DESCRIPTION

Adani Group: Adani Group is a diversified organisation in India comprising 10 publicly traded companies. It has created a world class logistics and utility infrastructure portfolio that has a pan-India presence. Adani Group is headquartered in Ahmedabad, in the state of Gujarat, India. Over the years, Adani Group has positioned itself to be the market leader in its logistics and energy businesses focusing on large scale infrastructure development in India with O & M practices benchmarked to global standards. With four IG rated businesses, it is the only Infrastructure Investment Grade issuer in India.

Adani Airports Holding Limited: Adani Airports Holding Limited is reshaping the aviation landscape, transforming airports into dynamic hubs of connectivity, innovation, and service excellence. By prioritizing passenger experience, leveraging state-of-the-art technology, and enhancing operational efficiency, we are redefining airport infrastructure across India. Our vision is to create world-class airports that serve as gateways to regional development and global connectivity, positioning Adani Airports as a leader in the aviation sector with a focus on sustainable growth and community impact.

Job Purpose: Screener is responsible to ensure the safe and efficient screening of hold baggage, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards and the detection of prohibited items or security threats. The Screener will be responsible for operating screening equipment, documenting findings, collaborating with other security staff, and interacting with passengers to maintain the highest standards of security.

Responsibilities

Baggage Screening and Threat Detection:

Attend all the regulatory trainings and clear trainings as per the regulatory attempts

Operate X-BIS equipment for image analysis to detect Dangerous Goods, Prohibited Articles, Weapons, or Ammunition in check-in baggage as per Avsec circulars/ orders /guidelines and SOPs.

Perform physical inspections and ETD tests for baggage flagged during X-ray screening.

Maintain a high standard of TIP (Threat Image Projection) scores to reflect strong image interpretation capabilities.

Compliance With Aviation Security Directives

Follow BCAS and DGCA guidelines to ensure strict compliance during baggage handling and inspection tasks.

Record and document all findings and operations to meet audit and inspection requirements.

Equipment Maintenance And Pre-Operational Checks

Conduct routine pre-operational checks of X-BIS, ETD, CT-EDS other security equipment to confirm their readiness and functionality.

Report equipment malfunctions or downtime and assist in maintaining operational registers.

Stakeholder Interaction And Coordination

Work collaboratively with CISF and Airlines staff during physical baggage inspections or suspicious item resolutions.

Provide timely updates to supervisors about ongoing operations or issues that may require intervention.

Time Management And Efficiency

Ensure baggage screening and inspection are completed within strict timelines to avoid operational delays.

Balance thoroughness and speed to maintain operational flow without compromising safety.

Workplace Conduct And Decorum

Maintain professionalism and discipline at all times, contributing positively to the team environment.

Uphold organizational standards in workplace behavior, ensuring compliance with company policies.

Key Stakeholders - Internal

CSO

Lead Avsec Audit & Compliance

Pass Section

Lead - ILHBS/ Duty Manager - ILHBS/ Sr Screener

Terminal Operations

IT

HR Department

Compliance and Regulatory Teams

Other Airport Departments (e.g., Operations, Customer Service)

Key Stakeholders - External

Transportation Security Administration (TSA)

Aircraft Operator

Airport Authorities

Passengers

ASG

BCAS

Local Police

Service Providers

Law Enforcement Agencies

Regulatory Bodies

Industry Associations

Qualifications

Educational Qualifications:

High school diploma or equivalent.

Bachelor’s or equivalent degree

Certifications: TSA certification required; Desirable to have cleared AVSEC basic course by BCAS, standalone / Inline Screeners certification by BCAS, DGR CAT 12 certification by DGCA Approved training center

NCC – “C” or “B” Certificate Holder will be preferred

Work Experience

Freshers or 1-2 years of experience in security screening or a related field, preferably within an airport setting.

Experience operating screening equipment and familiarity with security protocols.

Physical Qualification

Visual accuracy - Eyesight shall be equal or better than 20/60 in both eyes without glasses /

lenses or equal or better that 20 / 20 in one eye and 20 / 30 in the other when using glasses

or contact lenses. Adequate color perception shall be required.

Hearing – Hearing loss in each ear should be less than 30 dB when tested by audio graph for

frequencies below 2000 Hz.

Speech – There shall be clear ability to communicate orally efficiently and effectively.

Movement-of-crisis communication necessitates that screening personnel be free from any

pronounced speed impediment.

Sense of smell – Security personnel shall have the ability to sense Oduor

Deportment & Appearance - Security personnel must demonstrate a high standard of civility,

deportment, appearance and sobriety as well as offer the public all appropriate courtesies.

Bachelor’s or equivalent degree

items.title: ILHBS - Screener
items.description: ResponsibilitiesImage Interpretation of baggageMaintain rotation at X-BIS for smooth operation.Ensure 100% Compliance of BCAS, DGCA directives and guidelinesInitiatives taken to maintain decorum of workplaceMaintenance of all relevant register to ensure serviceability of equipment downtime monitoring & reporting.Conduct pre-operational checks of X-BIS, ETD, CT-EDS etc. meticulously and recorded for checking by Supervisor.Maintain discipline within the team.Maintain good TIP recordSmooth performance at location of ILHBSQualificationsDiploma with good communication skillsMinimum 1 Year of experience in screening and other security functions associated with Security department of Regulated Agent or Aircraft Operator or Airport Operator or ASGStandalone / Inline Screeners certification by BCAS DGR CAT 12 certification by DGCA Approved training center
items.descriptionHtml:
Responsibilities

  • Image Interpretation of baggage
  • Maintain rotation at X-BIS for smooth operation.
  • Ensure 100% Compliance of BCAS, DGCA directives and guidelines
  • Initiatives taken to maintain decorum of workplace
  • Maintenance of all relevant register to ensure serviceability of equipment downtime monitoring & reporting.
  • Conduct pre-operational checks of X-BIS, ETD, CT-EDS etc. meticulously and recorded for checking by Supervisor.
  • Maintain discipline within the team.
  • Maintain good TIP record
  • Smooth performance at location of ILHBS

Qualifications

  • Diploma with good communication skills
  • Minimum 1 Year of experience in screening and other security functions associated with Security department of Regulated Agent or Aircraft Operator or Airport Operator or ASG
  • Standalone / Inline Screeners certification by BCAS DGR CAT 12 certification by DGCA Approved training center

items.title: Senior Manager - Finance
items.description: ResponsibilitiesLead annual Financial budgeting, forecasting, and financial planning processes Manage OPEX planning, tracking, and optimizationOversee financial accounting operations including reporting and audits Perform variance analysis and recommend corrective actions Drive cost optimization initiatives Ensure financial controls, governance, and compliance Support strategic decision-making and investment evaluation Coordinate with auditors and internal stakeholders Key Skills & CompetenciesBudgeting, forecasting, and financial analysis Financial accounting and compliance Analytical and problem-solving skills Stakeholder management QualificationsQualifications & Experience Qualified Chartered Accountant (CA) - Mandatory 8–12 years of relevant experience in Finance, preferably in infrastructure or aviation sectors
items.descriptionHtml:
Responsibilities

  • Lead annual Financial budgeting, forecasting, and financial planning processes
  • Manage OPEX planning, tracking, and optimization
  • Oversee financial accounting operations including reporting and audits
  • Perform variance analysis and recommend corrective actions
  • Drive cost optimization initiatives
  • Ensure financial controls, governance, and compliance
  • Support strategic decision-making and investment evaluation
  • Coordinate with auditors and internal stakeholders

Key Skills & Competencies

  • Budgeting, forecasting, and financial analysis
  • Financial accounting and compliance
  • Analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Stakeholder management

Qualifications

Qualifications & Experience

  • Qualified Chartered Accountant (CA) - Mandatory
  • 8–12 years of relevant experience in Finance, preferably in infrastructure or aviation sectors
